// A type named System declared in this file shadows java.lang.System, so the
// scoped spelling must fail closed: an in-file decoy cannot impersonate the
// JDK facade.
class SystemLoggerDecoy {
    private static final System.Logger log = System.getLogger("x");

    void decoyDoesNotReport() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) { // want "JV001"
            log.log(System.Logger.Level.ERROR, "failed", e);
        }
    }

    void risky() throws Exception {}

    static class System {
        interface Logger {
            void log(Level lvl, String msg, Object... rest);
        }

        enum Level { ERROR }

        static Logger getLogger(String name) {
            return null;
        }
    }
}
